questions.scenario: The order service writes a customer, order header, line items, payment status, and fulfillment state during checkout. Support often asks questions that join these records together. Which storage choice best matches that shape?

options.text: Azure SQL Database, because relational records, constraints, and transactions fit this checkout data.
Blob Storage, because each order can be written as a JSON file.
Azure Files, because several processes might need a shared folder.
Managed Disks, because block storage keeps data close to a VM.

questions.explanation: Order records need relational consistency and queries across related facts. Azure SQL Database is the managed relational choice in this module.

questions.scenario: A deploy adds a nullable `coupon_code` column and a later release will start writing values. The team wants to avoid breaking old and new app versions during rollout. What review fits the article?

options.text: Check that the schema change is compatible while both app versions can run.
Skip database review because nullable columns cannot affect production behavior.
Move coupon data to Blob Storage until every server has the new code.
Restart the SQL server after deployment so old connections see the new column.

questions.explanation: Schema changes are part of deployment. The safe question is whether old and new application versions can both tolerate the database state during rollout.
